title: Fails to set 'security.selinux' extended file attribute when moving file inside container
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Fails to set 'security.selinux' extended file attribute when moving a file to a mapped volume inside a container.

This causes Python's `shutil.move()` to raise an exception and commandline `mv` to output an error message.
I'm not sure if this is actually a Podman bug but version 1.7.0 exhibits this while 1.6.2 doesn't.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. On Fedora 31: `mkdir ~/d`

2a. `podman run -i -t -w /root -v ~/d:/root:rw,z fedora:31 bash -c 'touch /tmp/f && python3 -c "import shutil;shutil.move(\"/tmp/f\", \"f\")"'`
or
2b. `podman run -i -t -w /root -v ~/d:/root:rw fedora:31 bash -c 'touch /tmp/f && mv /tmp/f .'`


**Describe the results you received:**
In both 2a and 2b the file is actually copied, but:

2a:

> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "/usr/lib64/python3.7/shutil.py", line 566, in move
>     os.rename(src, real_dst)
> OSError: [Errno 18] Invalid cross-device link: '/tmp/f' -> 'f'
> 
> During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:
> 
> Traceback (most recent call last):
>   File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
>   File "/usr/lib64/python3.7/shutil.py", line 580, in move
>     copy_function(src, real_dst)
>   File "/usr/lib64/python3.7/shutil.py", line 267, in copy2
>     copystat(src, dst, follow_symlinks=follow_symlinks)
>   File "/usr/lib64/python3.7/shutil.py", line 209, in copystat
>     _copyxattr(src, dst, follow_symlinks=follow)
>   File "/usr/lib64/python3.7/shutil.py", line 165, in _copyxattr
>     os.setxattr(dst, name, value, follow_symlinks=follow_symlinks)
> PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: 'f'

2b:

> mv: setting attribute 'security.selinux' for 'security.selinux': Permission denied

**Describe the results you expected:**
I expected the file to be moved without any errors.

**Additional information:**
Moving files from `/tmp` to `/tmp`, from `/root` to `/root` or from `/root` to `/tmp` doesn't cause any problems.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.7.0
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.13.5

  Distribution:

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 2146693120
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.7.0-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
The above info is from a VirtualBox Fedora 31 VM but it can be reproduced on a physical Fedora 31 machine too. I haven't tried reproducing it on other platforms.

comments.body: I assume you're running rootless?
@rhatdan Does this sound like a permissions issue because rootless can't set SELinux contexts, or is this more likely to be fuse-overlay?

The problem is shutil.move python bindings is attempting to copy the Security attributes from the source file to the target file.  I believe the latest shutil.copy code ignores this error, since it will not be allowed within a container.  Since SELinux indicates that it is disabled to processes within the container, shutil should not be attempting to keep these labels.

> I assume you're running rootless?

Yes, I am.

> The problem is shutil.move python bindings is attempting to copy the Security attributes from the source file to the target file. I believe the latest shutil.copy code ignores this error, since it will not be allowed within a container. Since SELinux indicates that it is disabled to processes within the container, shutil should not be attempting to keep these labels.

So what you are saying is that it is a bug in `shutil.move`?

I am saying, I believe in Python3 latest release in Fedora 31, shutil.move has been changed to ignore these errors.
The problem is, there are SELinux Extended attributes on these files and shutil.move is attempting to create them on a fuse-overlay file system.  SELinux is blocking them from being created.  There is nothing Podman or SELinux or fuse-overlay can do to fix this.

> I am saying, I believe in Python3 latest release in Fedora 31, shutil.move has been changed to ignore these errors.

I don't think it has. I'm on Fedora 31 using `python3-3.7.6-1.fc31.x86_64` which I believe is the latest version.

I found [this old issue](https://bugs.python.org/issue1438480) which is still open but seems like it would have fixed my issue too.

Thanks!
Looks like the move calls move calls copystat which calls copyxattrs.
Yes. `errno.EACCES` is missing from the tuple of allowed errors raised by `os.setxattr()`.
Yes not sure why they would handle EPERM and EACCESS differently.
This is a bug in python3, we can not fix it from podman.
@rhatdan do you know if a python bug got filed? I'm running into this now and this closed issue is at the top of the search results pile.
No I am not sure if I did open one.
@giuseppe Didn't you have  a way to handle this in fuse-overlayfs as well?
we have a way to skip using xattrs, if `noxattrs=1` is specified in the mountopts then it should work

I am seeing this issue on a f31 host using the f32 container (Which I believe to be using Python 3.8). I have a python script that uses shutil.move() to move files created via tempfile (likely usually in tmpfs, /tmp) to a destination file.

In this case I am using rootless podman and declaring my storage as -v ~/foobar:/foobar:z -- but shutil calls are still causing selinux failures; the troubleshooter indicates it is denying the relabelto/relabelfrom permissions.

(I believe in this case, the container environment would be moving a file from a fuse-overlayfs /tmp to a mapped volume owned by a non-root user.)

I think what's going wrong in my case is that I am using `sample_permissions` to copy the permissions of a file I intend to overwrite onto the tempfile, and *then* I try to move it over a destination file. This causes relabelto/relabelfrom errors everywhere.

It makes sense to me that if I am replacing a file with certain xattrs that I will want the same xattrs to be present on it once replaced; I don't think Python has a bug here exactly. (Maybe the bug is that python throws instead of continuing gracefully, if anything.)

I'll have to look into the specifics of my case more -- maybe this is just a docfix, somewhere, or a blog article. Hope this comment helps future searchers.

--js
shutils ignores the errors in one place and then fails on them in another.  They should be ignored everywhere.

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.9/shutil.py", line 803, in move
    os.rename(src, real_dst)
OSError: [Errno 18] Invalid cross-device link: '/var/tmp/mdapi-ggqz7n9j/epel-7_primary.sqlite' -> 'repositories/epel-7_primary.sqlite'
```
And what is the solution for F33?
Ask the python developers.
That's not just Python related. The same problem occurs while running a Fedora 34 container using Podman on a Fedora 34 system and calling the `mv` program from coreutils.

@giuseppe This bug still exists in podman 4 on Fedora 36 WS beta when rootless user tries to create container. There is no python here, only podman and crun. Container layer can't be created by btrfs storage driver. podman has stopped to work in Fedora 35 and never come back. I already opened issue #https://github.com/containers/storage/issues/1082 on container-storage but never got solution. If the solution was found 2 years ago could it be encapsulated and replicated to all containers packages runnable as rootless?

> @giuseppe This bug still exists in podman 4 on Fedora 36 WS beta when rootless user tries to create container. There is no python here, only podman and crun.

so that is a different issue.  Let's continue the discussion in https://github.com/containers/storage/issues/1082
